content.text:
I'll design a powder diffraction beamline with the specified requirements. Let me work through the optical design systematically.

## Design Rationale

**Source:** U2.5 undulator (period 21 mm, 219 periods) at 6 GeV. Design energy 12 keV (typical for powder diffraction).

**Energy Resolution ~0.1% (ΔE/E ~ 10⁻³):** A Si(111) DCM delivers ΔE/E ≈ 1.4×10⁻⁴, comfortably meeting the requirement.

**Focusing to 20 µm (H) × 10 µm (V):** Kirkpatrick-Baez pair of elliptical mirrors at the end of the beamline. The vertical mirror is placed further upstream (longer q) with a higher demagnification to achieve the tighter 10 µm focus; the horizontal mirror is closer to the sample with parameters tuned for 20 µm.

**Layout (cumulative distances from source):**

| Element | Distance from source | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Undulator | 0 m | Source |
| Primary slits | 14 m | Define acceptance, reduce heat load |
| DCM crystal 1 | 20 m | Si(111) monochromator, 1st crystal |
| DCM crystal 2 | 20.02 m | Si(111) monochromator, 2nd crystal (fixed offset) |
| Secondary slits | 28 m | Clean scatter, define coherence |
| KB Vertical mirror | 40 m | Elliptical, focuses vertically, p=40 m, q=5 m, demag=8:1 → ~10 µm |
| KB Horizontal mirror | 43 m | Elliptical, focuses horizontally, p=43 m, q=2 m, demag=21.5:1 → ~20 µm |
| Sample screen | 45 m | Powder diffractometer position |

The KB mirrors operate at 3 mrad grazing incidence (suitable for Rh or Pt coating at 12 keV). The vertical focusing mirror (VFM) at 40 m with q=5 m gives a demagnification of 8:1, producing ~10 µm vertical focus from the ~80 µm source. The horizontal focusing mirror (HFM) at 43 m with q=2 m gives demagnification of ~21.5:1, producing ~20 µm horizontal from the ~430 µm source (accounting for slit-defined effective source).

## Design Summary

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---|
| **Source** | U2.5 undulator, 6 GeV, 200 mA, 4.6 m long |
| **Design energy** | 12 keV (tunable via K and DCM angle) |
| **Monochromator** | Si(111) DCM at 20 m — ΔE/E ≈ 1.4×10⁻⁴ (well within 0.1%) |
| **KB VFM** | Elliptical at 40 m, 3 mrad grazing, p=40 m, q=5 m (demag 8:1 → ~10 µm V) |
| **KB HFM** | Elliptical at 43 m, 3 mrad grazing, p=43 m, q=2 m (demag 21.5:1 → ~20 µm H) |
| **Total length** | 45 m |
| **Focal spot** | ~20 µm (H) × ~10 µm (V) FWHM |

**Key design choices:**
- **Si(111) DCM** is the workhorse for powder diffraction — its intrinsic resolution (1.4×10⁻⁴) exceeds the 0.1% specification while maintaining high throughput
- The **KB geometry** decouples horizontal and vertical focusing, enabling independent optimization of the asymmetric 20×10 µm² spot
- **3 mrad grazing angle** on both KB mirrors ensures high reflectivity with Rh or Pt coating across the 8–25 keV powder diffraction range
- The **VFM placed before the HFM** gives the longer lever arm needed for the tighter vertical focus, while the shorter HFM-to-sample distance relaxes the horizontal demagnification
